title: Man rides out tornado inside van. See the footage

title: What pet cemeteries reveal about being human

title: These are the animals people most want to see photographed

title: How long will the cold weather last?
content: Friday feels noticeably colder. Helen Willetts explains why there's been a change in temperature and looks at how long it may last.

title: Lost Doctor Who episodes found in 'eclectic' collection
content: The Doctor's assistant Peter Purves was invited to a screening of the episodes in Leicester.

title: Woman only found out she had terminal brain cancer after a suitcase fell on her head
content: Lauren Macpherson was travelling home from a festival in London when her life changed forever.

title: Mission accomplished? The 2003 boast that haunts today's Iran conflict
content: The echoes between the conflicts are certainly there but there are also profound differences.

title: 'I missed my chemo and have a £12,000 hotel bill': British holidaymakers stranded by Iran war
content: Flights are restricted due to the conflict leaving people stuck running up bills for rooms and food.

title: BBC spends day with emergency teams as Israel strikes south Lebanon
content: The BBC visited the city of Nabatieh, witnessing the pace and scale of Israel's bombing as its war with Hezbollah continues to escalate.

title: What on earth is going on with the oil price?
content: Oil price moves have made headlines since the Iran conflict started - but why have there been such sharp swings?

title: 'There's no hiding place on a ship': The sailors stranded near Iran
content: Drones, cruise missiles and fighter jets have become a common sight for many sailors stranded on ships in the Gulf.

title: What do Americans think about Iran strikes?
content: Six US voters tell the BBC how they feel about the major US military intervention in Iran.

title: Tracking the wave of ship attacks that has choked off Strait of Hormuz
content: Six ships have been reportedly attacked in the Gulf in less than 48 hours, bringing the total vessels attacked during the war to 18.

title: I was jailed while pregnant during Post Office scandal. I'm still waiting for compensation
content: Seema Misra calls for accountability as a report by MPs raises concerns about ongoing delays.

title: French vote tests polarised electorate with right hoping to win control of Paris
content: The highest-profile contest is for the mayorship of Paris - which has been under left-wing control for 25 years.

title: Weekly quiz: This dog was Best in Show at Crufts - what other prize did he win?
content: How much attention did you pay to what happened in the world over the past seven days?

title: The US may move some of its anti-missile system - and it's sparking unease in South Korea
content: Reports say the US may be moving parts of its Thaad system to the Middle East, despite Seoul's opposition.

title: King expressed 'concern' over Alberta separatists, say First Nations chiefs
content: Indigenous leaders made Charles aware of the "threat" posed by the movement during a meeting in London, they say.

title: Kaye Adams denies 'malicious stories' after BBC misconduct complaint
content: The presenter was taken off air on BBC Radio Scotland last year following an internal complaint about her conduct.

title: Artemis II: Nasa targets early April for Moon mission
content: Nasa says technical problems that have delayed the rocket are fixed and it is ready for launch.

title: Romance conman posed as MI6 worker to scam women out of £50k
content: "Selfish and narcissistic" Rodney Ade Roberts is jailed for more than five years for scamming three women.

title: Mayor to consider new charges for SUVs in London
content: The plan is part the mayor's strategy to stop death and serious injury on London's roads by 2041.

title: BBC News app
content: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

title: Taking Back Control: Why ‘Agency’ Could Be The Next Big Idea In Politics
content: A top pollster explains how a sense of powerlessness amongst voters is shaping politics.

title: Iran’s New Supreme Leader (Sort Of) Speaks Out
content: Mojtaba Khamenei issues first message as Supreme Leader, but doesn’t appear in person.

title: Tobey Maguire stars as Spider-Man in the classic 2002 film
content: Peter Parker gains superpowers, but it takes a tragedy to teach him how to be a hero.

title: Russell takes dominant Chinese GP sprint pole
content: George Russell leads team-mate Kimi Antonelli as Mercedes dominate qualifying for the sprint race at the Chinese Grand Prix.

title: The numbers behind Borthwick's nightmare Six Nations
content: BBC Sport examines what has gone wrong with England's Six Nations campaign with their final game against France approaching.

title: Fastest to 100 wins - Europa specialist Emery sets Villa record
content: Unai Emery becomes the quickest of the three Aston Villa managers to reach 100 victories as his side defeat Lille in the Europa League last 16.

title: Draper beaten after controversial hindrance call
content: Jack Draper is penalised with a controversial hindrance call as Daniil Medvedev ends the Briton's Indian Wells title defence in the quarter-finals.

title: 'Iran's women are heroes - they want to be free'
content: The Iran women's national football team have become "heroes" and deserve to be free to live their lives, says former Iran men's head coach Afshin Ghotbi.

title: Sutton's predictions v YouTuber Angry Ginge
content: BBC Sport football expert Chris Sutton takes on YouTuber Angry Ginge plus the BBC readers and AI with his predictions for this weekend's Premier League fixtures on Tuesday, Wednesday and Thursday.

title: Why did market crash? Nifty50 closes at 23,151, Sensex down over 1,400 points
content: Stock market crash today: Continuing the down trend, Nifty50 and BSE Sensex tanked in trade on Friday as oil prices climbed and the Strait of Hormuz closure halted important global trade. While Nifty50 went below 23,200, BSE Sensex was down over 1,400 points.

title: Middle East crisis: 4 crew members killed after US refueling aircraft crashes in Iraq
content: A US KC-135 refueling aircraft crashed in western Iraq on Friday, resulting in the deaths of four of its six crew members. The US military confirmed the incident is under investigation but ruled out hostile or friendly fire. This marks the fourth US military aircraft loss in the Middle East conflict.

title: $3.5 a shot vs $3m Patriot: How high-energy lasers could replace missiles against Iranian attacks
content: High-energy lasers are emerging as a cost-effective defense against drones and missiles, with costs as low as $3.50 per shot compared to millions for missile interceptors. Despite decades of development and some deployments, challenges remain in scaling the technology for widespread military use due to environmental limitations and production hurdles.

title: Palantir CEO Alex Karp admits using Anthropic's Claude after Pentagon ban; says: Our products are integrated with Anthropic, and in future ...
content: Palantir CEO Alex Karp confirmed continued use of Anthropic's Claude AI models despite the Pentagon labeling the company a supply chain risk.  The Department of Defense is planning a phase-out, but acknowledges a transition will take time, with potential exemptions for mission-critical operations. This highlights the challenges in disentangling deeply embedded AI systems from defense operations.

title: Middle East crisis: Jaishankar holds fourth call with Iranian FM; bilateral ties in focus
content: India's External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar held his fourth phone call with Iranian FM Abbas Araghchi, discussing bilateral ties and BRICS issues amidst Middle East tensions. The conversation followed recent discussions on the Strait of Hormuz and maritime security, with Iran blaming US actions for regional instability.

title: Hormuz miscalculation? Trump sideswiped by Iran's closure of Strait
content: Washington underestimated Iran's threat to close the Strait of Hormuz, causing global oil market jitters. Despite planning, economic fallout wasn't fully accounted for, leaving the US scrambling. While officials claim preparedness, limited options and high risks for naval escorts are evident. Iran signals the strait's closure remains a pressure tool.

title: Oppn submits notices seeking motion for CEC’s removal to both House today
GTA Online Community Series offers triple GTA$ and free vehicles
Afghanistan hits Pakistan's Kohat military fort in retaliatory drone strikes
content: Afghanistan retaliated against Pakistani strikes that killed four in Kabul and Kandahar. Afghan forces claimed to have targeted Pakistani military centers in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, including a fortress and command center, following alleged "night-time violations" along the disputed Durand Line. This escalation intensifies existing tensions between the two nations.

title: How LPG issue, oil price surge & Hormuz crisis may hit Indian industry: Explained in 10 points
content: The scarcity of commercial LPG has emerged after shipments from the Gulf region were disrupted amid the ongoing conflict in West Asia. India relies heavily on imports from this region for its LPG requirements.

title: Who is Vanshika Chadha? Meet Kuldeep Yadav’s bride-to-be
content: Indian cricketer Kuldeep Yadav will marry his longtime partner Vanshika Chadha on March 14 in Mussoorie. The couple, childhood friends from Kanpur, saw their friendship grow into a lifelong relationship. Vanshika works at the Life Insurance Corporation of India and maintains a private life away from the spotlight. Wedding festivities begin March 13, with cricket stars like Virat Kohli expected to attend.

title: LPG shortage: What Infosys and HCL Tech's advisory to employees says
content: IT giants Infosys and HCLTech are implementing temporary measures due to an LPG gas shortage impacting India. Infosys has adjusted food court menus and cooking methods across its Bengaluru, Pune, and Chennai campuses. HCLTech has advised its Chennai employees to work from home as cafeteria vendors face operational challenges.

title: 'Proved misbehaviour': Oppn moves to impeach poll chief; 193 MPs sign notice
If Iran pull out of FIFA World Cup, Here's why Iraq and UAE could replace them
content: Iran's participation in the upcoming FIFA World Cup is uncertain due to geopolitical tensions with the United States, a co-host. Citing security and political concerns, Iranian authorities are contemplating withdrawal. If Iran pulls out, FIFA will need to select a replacement, likely from the Asian Football Confederation, with UAE and Iraq being potential candidates.

title: 'Nobody will assign them responsibilities': Why Supreme Court rejected plea on menstrual leave
content: The Supreme Court declined to mandate menstrual leave for women students and employees nationwide, citing concerns about potential negative impacts on women's employment and the risk of reinforcing gender stereotypes. The court emphasized that policy decisions of this nature should be made by government policymakers after consulting stakeholders, rather than through judicial intervention.

title: From cheering low gas cost to taking pride 'when oil prices go up': Trump’s evolving stance
content: President Trump's stance on rising oil prices has shifted, now framing them as beneficial for the US as a major oil producer. This change follows strikes against Iran, which triggered a surge in global oil and petrol prices, impacting the Strait of Hormuz. The administration is grappling with economic consequences and political considerations ahead of midterm elections.

title: This tiny village in Maharashtra has a magical secret that grows inside pumpkins
content: Miraj, a historic city in Maharashtra, is renowned for its musical instrument making. Craftsmen create exquisite sitars and tanpuras using a special type of pumpkin. This tradition, passed down through generations, faces threats from climate change impacting pumpkin size. The craft requires government support for survival. The disappearance of this art would mean losing music, history, and ancestral voices.

title: Kuldeep Yadav wedding: Chahal arrives in Dehradun, says ‘I will dance a lot’
content: Yuzvendra Chahal arrived in Dehradun for Kuldeep Yadav's wedding in Mussoorie, expressing excitement for the festivities. The spinner is set to tie the knot with fiancée Vanshika on March 14 at the Welcomhotel The Savoy Mussoorie. The event is anticipated to be a private yet star-studded affair with several prominent cricketers expected to attend.

title: Tharoor lauds PM Modi's outreach to Iran president, holding talks
content: Congress MP Shashi Tharoor praised Prime Minister Modi and EAM Jaishankar for engaging with Iran amidst escalating West Asian tensions. He urged a collective push for peace, highlighting the risks to Indian citizens in the Gulf and the nation's role as a constructive voice.

title: LPG crisis in India: How states are tackling supply disruptions from Iran war and Hormuz crisis
Eight weeks of bloodbath: Is Nifty IT facing a pause or the start of a bigger tide?
content: India's IT stocks are facing an eighth consecutive week of losses, wiping out significant market value and sparking debate. While some fear AI's disruptive impact, others see a buying opportunity amid historical rebounds. Foreign investors have exited, but a domestic fund is increasing its IT holdings, betting on a recovery.

title: 'Vital partner in keeping oil prices stable': US envoy lauds India amid Strait of Hormuz crisis
content: US envoy Sergio Gor voiced grave concerns over escalating tensions at the Strait of Hormuz, warning of potential global oil supply disruptions. He highlighted Iran's actions targeting commercial vessels, including those with Indian casualties, and stressed India's crucial role in maintaining stable oil prices.

title: Agra woman ends life, blames UP cop for false marriage promise in videos
content: A UP Police constable, JB Gautam, was arrested for allegedly abetting the suicide of a woman who accused him of mental harassment and breaking a marriage promise. The woman, who was living separately from her husband, reportedly met Gautam four years ago, and their relationship allegedly led to her distress after he refused to marry her.

title: Hundred top buy Coles' Rs 4.8 cr price tag won't even make IPL auction top 10
content: The Hundred 2026 auction saw significant spending, with James Coles topping the charts at Rs 4.8 crore. However, the IPL 2026 auction dwarfed these figures, with Cameron Green fetching a staggering Rs 25.20 crore.

title: 'These are plans to call women inferior': CJI led-SC bench rejects plea on menstrual leave
content: The Supreme Court has declined to mandate nationwide menstrual leave for women, citing concerns that it could reinforce gender stereotypes and harm employment prospects. While acknowledging voluntary policies are welcome, the court directed authorities to consider the petitioner's representation on the matter, emphasizing potential negative career impacts if made compulsory.

title: US launches 'forced labour' probe against India and 59 other countries: What is Section 301?
content: The US trade representative's office has launched Section 301 investigations into 60 economies, including India, for failing to address forced labor in imports. This move follows a Supreme Court ruling and aims to determine if foreign governments are sufficiently prohibiting goods made with forced labor, impacting US workers and businesses. New tariffs on several countries are possible by summer.

title: Sasikala announces new party name and symbol ahead of Tamil Nadu polls
US-Iran war: How Strait of Hormuz closure impacts global oil supply - explained in charts
content: The Strait of Hormuz, a critical energy route, is effectively blocked due to the escalating US-Israeli war on Iran, halting tankers traffic and forcing oil producers to cut the oil output. Damaged infrastructure and blocked shipping lanes are causing global supply shocks, leading to surging energy prices and economic strain worldwide.

title: First crewed Moon mission in over 50 years: Artemis II launch 'as early as April 1'
Income cannot be sole decider of OBC creamy layer: SC
content: The Supreme Court ruled that parental job status, not just income, must determine OBC 'creamy layer' exclusion. This decision impacts wards of PSU and private sector employees, aligning their criteria with government employees. The court emphasized that income alone is insufficient for exclusion, stressing the importance of job category and status.

title: Trump tariff ‘collapse’ triggers US trade probe — what it means for India
content: The economies included in the investigation are China, the European Union, Singapore, Switzerland, Norway, Indonesia, Malaysia, Cambodia, Thailand, South Korea, Vietnam, Taiwan, Bangladesh, Mexico, Japan and India.

title: Why Sunrisers Leeds bought Pakistan spinner at The Hundred auction: Vettori explains
content: Sunrisers Leeds' acquisition of Pakistani spinner Abrar Ahmed at The Hundred auction sparked controversy in India. Head coach Daniel Vettori clarified the move was a purely cricketing decision after missing out on Adil Rashid. Vettori emphasised that no internal discussions about avoiding Pakistani players occurred, focusing solely on the best available talent for the team's strategy.

title: Mumbai’s beloved samosa and vada pav disappear from menus as LPG supply tightens
content: Mumbai's beloved street food is facing an unprecedented crisis due to a severe shortage of commercial LPG cylinders. Popular eateries are struggling to operate, with some forced to close or drastically reduce their menus. Vendors are resorting to risky black market purchases to keep their businesses afloat, impacting daily staples for many Mumbaikars.

title: Crude above $100: How countries are racing to streamline oil supplies
content: Global oil markets are reeling from Middle East tensions, with the Strait of Hormuz facing disruptions.  Nations like India are securing Russian crude, while the US and other IEA members release emergency reserves. China eyes its reserves and Russia, as countries worldwide implement measures to stabilize prices and ensure supply.

title: Pocso case against sarpanch’s husband for sexual assault of minor in Telangana
content: A 27-year-old man, husband of a local sarpanch, has been booked under the POCSO Act for allegedly sexually assaulting a 17-year-old girl. The incident occurred on February 23 while the girl's daily wager parents were at work. The accused reportedly entered the house under the guise of counselling the minor, who had dropped out of college.

title: Did Iran hit US warship Abraham Lincoln? What we know
content: Iran's IRGC Navy claimed to have struck the USS Abraham Lincoln with ballistic missiles, rendering it non-operational. However, the US has denied this, stating the carrier continues its operations. Separately, a US Navy vessel reportedly fired on an Iranian ship that approached the Abraham Lincoln, with the Iranian vessel's status unknown.

title: Not Samson or Bumrah: Ponting gives England star 10/10 at T20 World Cup
content: Ricky Ponting lauded Will Jacks with a perfect 10, while Indian stars Sanju Samson and Jasprit Bumrah earned 9.5/10. Samson, Player of the Tournament, led India's charge with the bat, and Bumrah was a bowling maestro. India's dominant victory over New Zealand in the final cemented their status as three-time T20 World Cup champions.

title: Dubai targeted again: Video shows thick black smoke after blasts; debris hits building
Strait of Hormuz crisis: US waiver puts 19 m barrels of Russian oil back on market
content: A US waiver now permits the purchase of Russian oil cargoes already at sea, releasing nearly 30 tankers carrying millions of barrels of crude and refined products into the market. This move offers crucial breathing room for nations grappling with supply disruptions near the Strait of Hormuz, as China and India continue to acquire discounted Russian fuels.

title: Sirens heard at Turkey’s US-stationed Incirlik base, residents fear missile threat
content: Sirens wailed at Turkey's Incirlik airbase, a key NATO facility housing US troops, early Friday. Residents reported seeing smoke and a glowing object, with a red alert issued. This follows recent NATO interceptions of Iranian-fired missiles and heightened regional tensions.

title: 'We are totally destroying terrorist regime': Trump issues 'watch what happens today 'warning
content: President Trump declared a decisive campaign against Iran, vowing to "destroy the terrorist regime" militarily and economically. He asserted US military superiority, claiming Iran's capabilities are being "decimated."  Trump issued a stern warning to Tehran's leadership, highlighting the US's "unparalleled firepower" and his personal resolve as the 47th President to counter Iran's global actions.

title: Top 8 premium residential areas in Gurugram dominating the luxury real estate market
content: Gurugram is emerging as a prime luxury real estate market in India, attracting professionals and High Net-Worth Individuals. Areas like Golf Course Road, DLF phases, and Sohna Road offer a variety of high-end apartments and villas with modern amenities. The city's robust corporate culture and excellent connectivity further enhance its appeal as a top destination for upscale living.

title: Rapper Santy Sharma backs Badshah amid song lyrics controversy
content: Badshah's 'Tateeree' sparks outrage over vulgar lyrics and schoolgirl video, drawing Haryana police notice. He apologized, pulled the track, but stays silent amid backlash. Santy Sharma defends him, blaming video team and hip-hop misconceptions. Controversy rages on social media.

title: Pak bombs Kabul homes, Kandahar airport in renewed strikes; women, children killed - key details
content: Pakistan launched airstrikes on Afghanistan's capital, Kabul, early Friday, resulting in four deaths and 15 injuries. This latest escalation stems from Pakistan's accusations that the Afghan Taliban government harbors the Tehrik-e-Taliban Pakistan (TTP), which has increased attacks from Afghan territory.

title: /r/WorldNews Live Thread: Russian Invasion of Ukraine Day 1478, Part 1 (Thread #1625)
/r/WorldNews Discussion Thread: US and Israel launch attack on Iran; Iran retaliates (Thread #8)
content:
If you see any newsworthy information from a major news outlet or live broadcast, feel free to share a brief summary as a top-level comment in the discussion post.

Other redditors will appreciate if you include the source of where you read, saw, or heard the information.
